snippet.title: CEO Series: Mary Barra of General Motors on Committing to an Eco-Friendly Future | HBR IdeaCast
snippet.description:
Mary Barra, chair and CEO of General Motors, says that electric vehicles are the future for the company and the automobile industry. GM has said it will phase out vehicles using internal combustion engines by 2035 and go carbon neutral at all of its facilities. Barra describes how she’s executing on that plan as well as offering broader leadership lessons in an interview with HBR editor Amy Bernstein.

This episode originally aired on HBR IdeaCast on May 6, 2021.

Listen to more HBR IdeaCast episodes here: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLzAU8TPKsJuaxff5Cp0P2DKE_tFyaWOOa

snippet.title: How Mary Barra makes $14.6M per year as the CEO of General Motors
snippet.description: Join us for an inspiring journey into the life of Mary Barra, CEO of General Motors. From a factory floor worker captivated by cars to the first woman leading a major automobile manufacturer, Mary's story is one of determination and groundbreaking achievements. Discover her rise from humble beginnings, driven by an unwavering pursuit of knowledge and a commitment to understanding every aspect of the automotive business. Witness her exceptional crisis management during the GM ignition switch scandal, turning adversity into an opportunity for safety and responsibility. Explore her visionary leadership in driving innovation and sustainability, propelling General Motors into the forefront of electric and autonomous vehicles. Experience her compassionate and inclusive leadership style, from her swift response to the COVID-19 pandemic to achieving gender pay equity within the company. Join us in celebrating Mary Barra's extraordinary journey that inspires individuals worldwide.

snippet.title: Mary Barra has RUINED GM! But General Motors CEO just SHOCKED everyone!
snippet.description:
Join us as we delve into the remarkable journey of Mary Barra, the CEO of General Motors (GM), and discover the challenges she has faced and conquered during her tenure at one of the world's leading automotive companies.

In this inspiring video, we explore how Mary Barra has navigated the complex and ever-evolving landscape of the automotive industry, tackling significant obstacles head-on and implementing transformative changes to revitalize General Motors.

Witness how Barra's leadership prowess and strategic vision propelled her to the forefront of the industry, all while breaking barriers as the first female CEO of a major global automaker. We delve into the challenges she encountered, including the fallout from the 2014 ignition switch crisis, intense competition, and the rapid technological disruptions that continue to shape the future of transportation.

Discover how Mary Barra's unwavering determination and resilience enabled her to guide GM through these obstacles. Learn about her commitment to fostering a culture of innovation, spearheading the company's transition towards electric and autonomous vehicles, and forging key partnerships with tech giants and startups to stay ahead of the curve.

Through interviews with industry experts and insiders, we uncover the strategic decisions and transformative initiatives that Barra championed, ensuring General Motors not only survives but thrives in an era of unprecedented change.

Join us on this compelling journey as we explore Mary Barra's challenges, triumphs, and the indelible mark she has left as CEO of General Motors, transforming the company into a forward-thinking powerhouse ready to shape the future of mobility.

Prepare to be inspired by the resilience and visionary leadership of Mary Barra, a trailblazer who continues to redefine the automotive landscape and drive General Motors towards an electrifying future.

Tags: #elonmusk , #innovation, @Manatek, #interview 

Welcome to our exclusive conversation with Mary Barra, CEO of General Motors, and Rebecca Blumenstein, famous writer and Deputy Managing Editor of The New York Times. In this riveting talk, we dive into Mary Barra's creative and revolutionary efforts, as well as Rebecca Blumenstein's observations on the changing face of the automotive business.

Join us as we investigate Mary Barra's imaginative leadership, which has catapulted General Motors to the forefront of technical innovations, sustainability, and electrification. Discover her strategy approach to directing the organization towards a cleaner, greener future while assuring great quality and customer happiness. This interview promises to reveal the major driving reasons behind General Motors' success, from electrification of their car portfolio to significant developments in autonomous driving.

In addition, Rebecca Blumenstein, a well-known journalist, provides her experience to shed light on the larger implications of General Motors' transformational path. Gain vital insights into the nexus of the automotive sector and the ever-changing global market dynamics. Rebecca's extensive background in business, technology, and foreign politics allows her to bring a unique perspective on General Motors' current difficulties and prospects.

Mary Barra's unrelenting commitment to innovation, sustainability, and inclusive leadership will inspire you as she highlights the company's progressive initiatives and commitment to diversity and equity. Rebecca Blumenstein's keen insights and probing questions will ensure a stimulating discussion on the larger implications of General Motors' strategic decisions.

snippet.title: How can GM CEO be THIS BAD and still have a job!? | The SHOCKING truth about Mary Barra
snippet.description:
In this thought-provoking video, we delve into the controversial tenure of Mary Barra, the CEO of General Motors (GM), and examine the alleged missteps that led to the decline of one of America's most iconic automotive companies. Join us as we dissect the decisions, strategies, and management style of Mary Barra, questioning whether her leadership has indeed contributed to running GM into the ground.

Throughout the video, we present a comprehensive analysis of the major controversies and challenges that have plagued General Motors under Barra's leadership. From the massive ignition switch recall crisis to the decline in product quality and innovation, we explore the critical moments that have raised concerns among industry experts and GM enthusiasts alike.

Using a balanced approach, we also highlight some of the positive aspects of Barra's tenure, such as her efforts to promote electric vehicles and GM's entry into the autonomous vehicle market. However, we scrutinize the overall impact of these initiatives and evaluate whether they have been enough to counterbalance the company's ongoing struggles.

Through interviews with industry insiders, automotive journalists, and former GM employees, we provide unique perspectives on Mary Barra's management style, decision-making process, and ability to navigate the challenging landscape of the automotive industry. We invite viewers to reflect on the implications of her leadership choices and the long-term consequences for General Motors.

Ultimately, this video aims to foster a constructive dialogue about the role of leadership in shaping the fate of an organization. Whether you're an automotive enthusiast, a business-minded individual, or simply interested in the dynamics of corporate leadership, this video offers a compelling exploration of Mary Barra's impact on General Motors and raises critical questions about the company's future. Join us as we unravel the complexities and controversies surrounding Mary Barra's tenure as CEO of GM.

snippet.title: Tribute: Mary Barra - 2023 Larry Foster Award honoree
snippet.description:
Mary Barra is chair and chief executive officer of General Motors. Barra began her career with GM in 1980 as a co-op student at the Pontiac Motor Division. She is chair of the Business Roundtable, an association of chief executive officers of leading U.S. companies. She serves as the chair and founding member of GM’s inclusion advisory board. Barra also serves on the board of directors of the Walt Disney Company, the Duke University Board of Trustees and the Detroit Economic Club.

Barra was honored at the seventh annual Arthur W. Page Center Awards for Integrity in Public Communication on February 22, 2023. The event was virtual. Learn more: https://www.bellisario.psu.edu/page-center/events/page-center-awards

Mary Barra is chair and chief executive officer of General Motors. Barra began her career with GM in 1980 as a co-op student at the Pontiac Motor Division. She is chair of the Business Roundtable, an association of chief executive officers of leading U.S. companies. She serves as the chair and founding member of GM’s inclusion advisory board. Barra also serves on the board of directors of the Walt Disney Company, the Duke University Board of Trustees and the Detroit Economic Club.

snippet.title: EVs: Are We There Yet? | Episode 1: Hertz + GM
snippet.description:
Our CEO, Stephen Scherr, picks up the CEO of General Motors, Mary Barra, in the all-new Cadillac LYRIQ EV. The pair drives through the iconic GM campus, discussing the latest in electric vehicle innovation, their favorite driving playlists, and more. 

About "EVs: Are We There Yet?"

As Hertz builds one of the largest electric car rental fleets in the world, there’s one question Hertz CEO Stephen Scherr is often asked about electric travel – “Are we there yet?”

In this video series, he embarks on a cross-country EV road trip with a wide range of guests – from CEOs and thought leaders to sports figures and celebrities – to answer that question and discuss the possibilities of an electric future.

snippet.title: Is it Too Late for General Motors to Go Electric? | Freakonomics Radio | Episode 442
snippet.description:
G.M. produces more than 20 times as many cars as Tesla, but Tesla is worth nearly 10 times as much. Mary Barra, the C.E.O. of G.M., is trying to fix that. We speak with her about the race toward an electrified (and autonomous) future, China and Trump, and what it’s like to be the “fifth-most powerful woman in the world.”

This episode was originally published December 2, 2020.
For a full transcript, resources, and more, visit: https://freak.ws/3W5Uzj4
Photo credit: Audrey Bernstein

ABOUT FREAKONOMICS RADIO:
Discover the hidden side of everything with host Stephen J. Dubner, co-author of the Freakonomics books. Each week, Dubner speaks with Nobel laureates and provocateurs, intellectuals and entrepreneurs, and various other underachievers to uncover things you always thought you knew (but didn’t) and things you never thought you wanted to know (but do) — from the economics of sleep to the future of education, from markets for marriage to the surprising utility of wolves.

ABOUT THE FREAKONOMICS RADIO NETWORK:
Freakonomics began as a book, which led to a blog, a documentary film, more books, a pair of pants, and in 2010, a podcast called Freakonomics Radio. Hosted by Stephen J. Dubner, it’s one of the most popular podcasts in the world, with a reputation for storytelling that is both rigorous and entertaining. Its archive of more than 500 episodes is available, for free, on any podcast app, and the show airs weekly on NPR stations. Freakonomics Radio is now the flagship show of the Freakonomics Radio Network, which includes the podcasts No Stupid Questions, People I (Mostly) Admire, and The Economics of Everyday Things.
